name: Butternut Squash Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the squash into thick chunks and remove the seeds.
2. Place the squash in a baking dish and cover with water. Bake at 350°F (175°C) until tender, about 1 hour.
3. Using a food processor, blender, or food mill, puree the cooked squash until smooth.
4. Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the flour and cook briefly, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
5. Gradually add the milk while stirring continuously until the mixture thickens.
6. Stir in the ground ginger, cinnamon, salt, sugar, orange juice, and pureed squash.
7. Allow the mixture to cool slightly, then gently fold in the beaten egg yolks.
8. In a separate bowl, beat the 3 egg whites with a pinch of salt until stiff but not dry.
9. Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the squash mixture.
10. Spoon the soufflé batter into a 2-quart souffle dish and place it in a larger baking dish filled with water up halfway the side of the dish.
11. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 1-1/2 hours, or until set and golden brown on top. Test for doneness as you would for a cake.
12. The soufflé will stay fluffy for about 30 minutes after baking.
